question: The Orchestration Cluster REST API is part of the Camunda 8 public API and is covered by SemVer stability guarantees. According to the docs, which endpoints are excluded from that guarantee?

options.text: All endpoints under `/v2/` — only `/v1/` is stable.
Endpoints used by Operate or Tasklist UIs internally.
There are no exclusions; every endpoint is covered without exception.

a.text: The docs state the API is covered by SemVer stability guarantees "except for clearly marked alpha endpoints," so alpha endpoints are the documented exclusion.

b.text: The `/v2/` path is the current stable API; the docs do not exclude entire path prefixes from SemVer.

c.text: Internal UI consumers are irrelevant to the public-API stability contract.

d.text: There is an explicit exception for clearly marked alpha endpoints, so this is not accurate.

explanation: The page positions the Orchestration Cluster REST API as a stable public-API surface backed by Camunda's SemVer guarantees. The only carve-out is endpoints that are clearly marked as alpha — those can change without backward-compatibility guarantees so the team can iterate on new functionality before promoting it to stable.
